source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Which of the following objects can be obtained by rotating in space the grey object?
<image1>
<image2>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'the grey object' and asks about rotating it in space, which depends on visual shape/orientation information not contained in text alone
asset_registry_record shows an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving requires mental rotation of the reference object and comparison against multiple candidate objects
The answer is a subset selection ('W and Y'), so the solver must check more than one candidate rather than identify a single obvious match
This is visual-spatial reasoning with at least two stages: transform the grey object mentally, then verify matches/non-matches among the labeled options
verifiability: Closed-set multiple-choice question with 5 explicit options
answer_text='W and Y' exactly matches choice A
answer_type is short_text, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true, making outcome checking straightforward

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, answer, and required image are all present.
Asset registry passed with no recorded issues, so there is no evident asset gap blocking recovery.
The sample has high image dependency and the image exists, which supports preserving this visually grounded item.
High verifiability score and explicit answer matching make the sample straightforward to check.
Multi-step visual-spatial reasoning score is strong, indicating good candidate quality for the pool.
